Title: B - Homeostasis
Description: Module B

Title: B01 - Definition
Description: Definition

Title: AP-19-B-01-01
Description: Define homeostasis.

Description: Define the following terms as they relate to homeostasis: setpoint, variable, receptor (sensor), effector (target), and control (integrating) center.

Title: AP-19-B-01-03
Description: List the main physiological variables for which the body attempts to maintain homeostasis.

Title: AP-19-B-01-04
Description: *Explain the difference between a regulated variable and a controlled variable.

Description: Define the law of mass balance and relate it to body homeostasis.

Description: Compare and contrast equilibrium and steady-state.

Description: General types of homeostatic mechanisms

Description: List the steps in a feedback mechanism (loop) and explain the function of each step.

Description: Explain why negative feedback is the most common mechanism used to maintain homeostasis.

Title: AP-19-B-02-05

Description: For the important solutes of the body (e.g., Na+, K+, glucose, urea), describe how each segment of the nephron handles the solute and compare the filtration rate of the solute to its excretion rate (i.e., the net handling of the solute by the nephron). 2

Title: AP-19-P-04-12
Description: Explain the difference between clearance of a solute and renal handling of a solute.

Description: State the equation for clearance of a solute and explain how inulin and creatinine clearance are used to determine glomerular filtration rate (GFR).

Description: Describe the percentage of filtrate that is reabsorbed in each segment of the nephron.

Title: AP-19-P-04-15
Description: Trace the changes in filtrate osmolarity as it passes through the segments of the nephron.

Title: AP-19-P-04-16
Description: Explain the role of the nephron loop (of Henle), its permeability to water, and the high osmolarity of the interstitial fluid in the renal medulla in the formation of dilute urine. 3

Title: AP-19-P-04-17
Description: Describe the transport processes involved in eliminating drugs (e.g., penicillin), wastes, and foreign substances.

Title: AP-19-P-04-18
Description: Describe the composition of normal urine.

Title: P05 - Control of sodium, potassium, and water homeostasis
Description: Control of sodium, potassium, and water homeostasis

Description: Describe the signals that cause release of aldosterone from the adrenal cortex and the effect of aldosterone on the nephron, including the tubule segment involved and the transport mechanisms that are altered by aldosterone.

Title: AP-19-P-05-03
Description: Describe the effect of vasopressin (ADH, antidiuretic hormone) on the nephron and on the final concentration of urine.

Title: AP-19-P-05-04
Description: Describe the factors that cause release of natriuretic peptide hormones, their sites of synthesis, and their effects on the nephron.

Description: Additional endocrine activities of the kidney

Description: Describe the role of the kidney in vitamin D activation.

Title: AP-19-P-06-02
Description: Describe the role of the kidney in regulating erythropoiesis.

Title: AP-19-P-06-03
Description: Describe the effect of parathyroid hormone (PTH) on renal handling of calcium and phosphate.

Title: P07 - Micturition (urination)
Description: Micturition (urination)

Title: AP-19-P-07-02
Description: Describe voluntary control of micturition.

Description: Application of homeostatic mechanisms

Title: AP-19-P-08-01

Title: P09 - Predictions related to disruption of homeostasis
Description: Predictions related to disruption of homeostasis

Title: AP-19-Q-04-04
Description: Describe the integrated responses of the endocrine, digestive, skeletal, and urinary systems to disruptions of calcium homeostasis.

Title: Q05 - Acid-base homeostasis and buffer systems

Description: Describe the concept of compensation in relation to disruption of pH homeostasis.
